system: single ideal capacitor in declared charge coordinate

practical: capacitor voltage V = q / Ccap measured with a high-impedance voltmeter

control_variables.name: epsilon_abs
control_variables.meaning: absolute permittivity when using a parallel-plate model

control_variables.name: A_plate
control_variables.meaning: plate area when using a parallel-plate model

control_variables.name: d_gap
control_variables.meaning: plate separation when using a parallel-plate model

regime_assumptions: charge q is the declared energy coordinate
leakage, dielectric nonlinearity, fringe fields, and measurement loading are outside this card
capacitance is treated as a fixed positive control parameter
coordinate_note: This card uses charge q as the coordinate. If voltage V is used as the coordinate, U(V) = 1/2 Ccap V^2 gives a different Hessian with different units; the two coordinates must not be compared as the same stiffness table.

module_object.description: scalar capacitor energy Hessian in charge coordinate

derivation_status: exact after declaring charge as the capacitor energy coordinate

ceiling_or_reference.description: no hidden coordinate is declared in this card

positive_scope: This card exposes capacitor storage as a scalar quadratic module object in a declared charge coordinate.
boundary: It does not describe voltage-coordinate geometry, leakage, nonlinear dielectrics, fringing fields, or dynamic RC/RLC response.
failure_mode: The attachment fails if capacitance is not fixed and positive over the operating regime or if the measurement surface cannot support the declared charge coordinate.
next_measurement: Measure capacitance and voltage, then infer q = Ccap V under high-impedance conditions.
